what_manipulated: availability of self-handicap (lack of study effort); direction of counterfactual thinking (upward vs. downward vs. none)
what_measured: self-esteem; future study intentions; upward counterfactual thoughts
experiment: Participants received failure feedback on an academic task. In Study 1, counterfactual thoughts were generated without instruction. In Study 2, presence/absence of a self-handicap (practice effort) was experimentally varied. In Study 3, direction of counterfactual thinking was manipulated (upward vs. downward vs. control), and subsequent preparation effort was recorded. Outcome measures assessed self-esteem and motivation to improve.

what_manipulated: whether protagonist tempted fate (presumptuousness or caution); subliminal prime presence and type
what_measured: perceived likelihood of negative outcome; reaction time to outcome accessibility; subjective likelihood judgments
experiment: Studies 1-2 assessed likelihood judgments following actions that tempt fate vs. cautious actions using scenario methods. Study 3 measured accessibility via response latencies to story endings. Study 4 measured both accessibility and likelihood judgments. Study 5 manipulated accessibility via subliminal priming (negative outcome prime vs. control) while varying fate-tempting behavior. Study 6 used cognitive load to test System 1 involvement. Outcome was perceived likelihood of negative consequences.

what_manipulated: expressions of vulnerability (measured); reflected appraisals of vulnerability (manipulated in Study 5)
what_measured: authenticity doubts; perceived rejection; partner derogation
experiment: Study 1 assessed prototypes about vulnerable individuals and expected inauthentic responding via open-ended and rating measures. Studies 2A-2B tested whether expressed vulnerabilities predict reflected appraisals of vulnerability, which in turn predict authenticity doubts. Study 3 tested link between authenticity doubts and perceived rejection. Study 4 used longitudinal data to test the full mediation model. Study 5 experimentally manipulated reflected appraisals of vulnerability in a new interaction context. Mediation pathways were tested statistically.

what_manipulated: color of background or clothing (red vs. white, gray, green, blue)
what_measured: perceived attractiveness; sexual desire; sexual behavior intentions; dating intentions; spending intentions
experiment: Experiment 1: participants viewed female photo on red vs. white background and rated attractiveness. Experiment 2: added female participants to test sex specificity. Experiments 3-5: varied color contrasts (red vs. gray/green/blue), extended measures to sexual attraction and behavioral intentions, tested specificity to attractiveness/attraction (not likeability/intelligence/kindness). Response times and awareness probes assessed unconscious processing. All experiments used between-subjects designs with random assignment to color condition.

Comprehensive meta-analytic review of 115 longitudinal studies representing over 45,000 marriages. Studies varied in design, duration, measures, and predictors. No experimental manipulation; review evaluated how marriages change over time and which baseline variables predict quality and stability trajectories. Theory and method integration examined.

what_measured: subjective well-being (life satisfaction, happiness, positive affect, negative affect); personality trait correlations
experiment: Meta-analytic synthesis of 148 studies yielding 1,538 correlations relating 137 distinct personality variables to subjective well-being. Examined whether personality traits differentially predict different SWB conceptualizations. Clustering of traits into Big Five factors examined. No experimental manipulation; correlational literature synthesized. Methodological moderators tested (sample characteristics, measurement delay, scale properties).
